knowledge_objects.name: Partial Derivative
knowledge_objects.short_definition: A partial derivative measures how a function changes when one coordinate changes while the other coordinates are held fixed.
knowledge_objects.source_span: A partial derivative measures how f changes when one coordinate changes while the other coordinates are held fixed.

knowledge_objects.name: Gradient
knowledge_objects.short_definition: The gradient collects partial derivatives into a vector and points in the direction of steepest local increase.
knowledge_objects.source_span: The gradient ∇f(x) collects these partial derivatives into a vector.

knowledge_objects.name: Directional Derivative
knowledge_objects.short_definition: For a unit direction u, the directional derivative is ∇f(x)·u.
knowledge_objects.source_span: For a unit direction u, the directional derivative is ∇f(x)·u

knowledge_objects.name: Stationary Point
knowledge_objects.short_definition: A point where the gradient is the zero vector is called a stationary point.
knowledge_objects.source_span: A point where the gradient is the zero vector is called a stationary point.

knowledge_objects.name: Taylor Approximation
knowledge_objects.short_definition: Taylor approximation describes the local behavior of a differentiable function near a point using polynomial approximations.
knowledge_objects.source_span: Taylor approximation describes the local behavior of a differentiable function near a point.

knowledge_objects.name: Hessian Matrix
knowledge_objects.short_definition: The Hessian matrix describes local curvature for twice differentiable functions.
knowledge_objects.source_span: the Hessian matrix, which describes local curvature
